theoretical.explanation: METIS multilevel algorithm has near-linear complexity. Coarsening: O(|E|), Initial partitioning: O(|V|log|V|), Refinement: O(|E|). Overall: O(|E| + |V|log|V|) per bisection. For k districts, log2(k) bisection levels, total O(k(|E| + |V|log|V|)).
theoretical.edge_weighting_overhead: Edge weight computation: O(|E|) to compute demographic edge weights. Added to METIS call, does not change asymptotic complexity. Empirical overhead: ~12%.
theoretical.scalability: Tract-level (1,000-2,000 nodes): ~0.15-1.0s per state. Block-level (50,000 nodes): Extrapolated ~15-30s per state (25x nodes --> ~30x runtime given near-linear scaling). National (all 50 states): Parallelizable, ~1-2 hours total.

scaling.note: Extrapolation assumes O(n log n) scaling from empirical tract-level performance.

experiment_cost.note: 105 total configurations tested (21 per state × 5 states).
